sentence_reviews.text: Intermediate computation is expanded in tokens, hidden states, or recurrent steps before committing to an answer.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA search/reasoning material, Transformer autoregressive sequence modeling, and test-time compute scaling evidence; latent-state wording is synthesized.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited sources support expanded intermediate computation in token/search settings, but the hidden-state and recurrent-step wording is broader than any one cited source.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning is to connect Decoder-only causal transformers, Autoregressive decoding to downstream capabilities including Self-consistency & best-of-N inference, Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning, ReAct-style reasoning and acting.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA reasoning/search material, Transformer autoregressive modeling, and test-time compute scaling evidence; downstream graph-role wording is synthesized.
sentence_reviews.notes: The graph connection is defensible but not stated exactly as this ontology sentence in the cited sources.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Test-time compute and sampled reasoning/selection evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: Self-consistency and best-of-N methods rely on multiple generated reasoning/output candidates, but the packet does not cite a dedicated self-consistency source.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA planning, problem solving, and hierarchical reasoning material.
sentence_reviews.notes: Intermediate reasoning can support decomposition, but the exact substrate wording is ontology synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for ReAct-style reasoning and acting
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA agent/reasoning/action material; ReAct-specific claim is not directly cited.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited source supports reasoning and acting in agents, but not the ReAct method specifically.

sentence_reviews.text: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Decoder-only causal transformers, Autoregressive decoding.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA search cost/error considerations, Transformer/autoregressive modeling, and test-time compute scaling evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: Cost growth is supported, but confident-error and weak-verifier wording is broader than the cited evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning uses Decoder-only causal transformers as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Transformer architecture and autoregressive sequence modeling evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: Supported for LLM chain-of-thought implementations, but chain-of-thought reasoning is not inherently limited to decoder-only transformers.

sentence_reviews.text: Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning uses Autoregressive decoding as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Autoregressive generation and test-time sampled reasoning evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly defensible for token-expanded chain-of-thought, but latent/recurrent reasoning variants may not use autoregressive decoding.

sentence_reviews.text: Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning uses In-context & test-time learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Test-time compute scaling evidence and AIMA reasoning/search material.
sentence_reviews.notes: Test-time computation is relevant, but calling in-context/test-time learning a reusable ingredient is broader than the cited evidence.

edge_reviews.notes: Supported for modern LLM chain-of-thought systems, but not intrinsic to all chain-of-thought or latent reasoning.
Token-level chain-of-thought uses autoregressive decoding; latent variants make the edge less universally exact.
The relationship is plausible for inference-time reasoning, but the packet evidence does not tightly establish this dependency as common.
